rules.rule: Variable names must fully spell out the concept — no abbreviations, single letters, or shortened names.
Collection variable names must be plural — must not be suffixed with List, Array, or a type name.
Variable names must not include the type as a suffix or prefix.
Variables holding a null or default value must be prefixed with no (e.g., noStudent, noChangeCount).
Use var when the right-hand side type is immediately clear from the expression.
Use explicit type when the right-hand side is a method call whose return type is not immediately visible.
Use var for anonymous types where no explicit type exists.
For single-property instantiation: declare then assign on the next line. Must not use an object initializer block for a single property.
For two or more properties: use an object initializer block.
Variable declarations exceeding 120 characters must break at the = sign, with the RHS indented on the next line.
Single-line declarations must be stacked with no blank lines between them.
Declarations that occupy two or more lines must be surrounded by one blank line above and one below.
